title: Yale - Creatine Kinase Activity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"di1ka","text":"Summary: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e18on","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fotcv","text":"Procedure used to determine the creatine kinase activity in blood , serum, and plasma. Creatine kinase activity is measured by the enzymatically coupled reactions of creatine kinase, hexokinase, and glucose-6-P dehydrogenase. The rate of NADPH formation is monitored by the change in absorbance at 340 nm. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Calibrate Cobas for the measurement of creatine kinase activity analysis by running two control serum.

Sample handling as performed by the Cobas Mira Plus. a) Pipette 4.5 µL of sample into a cuvette slot. b) Add 175 µL of CK NADP Imidazole Reagent. c) Mixture is incubated at 37ºC and spun for 10 minutes. d) Absorbance is measured at 340nm.
creator: Lili Liang
